requirement_text: Object model modules shall contain identification information sufficient to distinguish the module and its provenance
Object class structure shall preserve class hierarchy and inheritance relationships
Object class definitions shall expose declared and inherited attributes consistently through the active catalog
Interaction class structure shall preserve class hierarchy and inheritance relationships
Interaction class definitions shall expose declared and inherited parameters consistently through the active catalog
Attribute tables shall preserve attribute names and availability for object class lookup
Parameter tables shall preserve parameter names and availability for interaction class lookup
Dimension tables shall preserve routing-space dimension names for active region and DDM use
Dimension names shall be unique within the object model
Time representation tables shall preserve logical time implementation selection from the active model
Object models shall support declaration of synchronization points
Synchronization point names shall be unique within the object model
Parser shall preserve synchronization point metadata
Validator shall reject duplicate synchronization point definitions
Synchronization definitions shall survive parse/serialize round-trip
Object models shall define transportation types
Transportation type names shall be unique
Parser shall preserve declared transportation type names
Attributes shall reference only valid transportation types
Interaction classes shall reference only valid transportation types
Validator shall reject references to undefined transportation types
Object models shall support update rate designators
Update rate designator names shall be unique
Parser shall preserve declared update rate names and values
Attributes referencing update rate designators shall reference valid entries
Validator shall reject undefined update rate references
Object models shall support declaration of switch metadata
Parser shall preserve declared switch settings
Validator shall ensure switch references are resolvable
Object models shall support datatype table declarations
Basic datatype names shall be unique
Parser shall preserve endian size encoding and semantics metadata
Validator shall reject duplicate datatype definitions
Parser shall preserve declared datatype names from datatype tables
Parser shall preserve datatype names referenced by attributes and parameters
Object models shall support simple datatype aliases
Simple datatypes shall reference valid underlying datatypes
Validator shall reject unresolved datatype references
Object models shall support enumerated datatypes
Enumeration names shall be unique
Enumeration values shall be unique within an enumeration
Parser shall preserve enumeration literal ordering
Validator shall reject duplicate enumeration values
Object models shall support fixed arrays
Object models shall support variable arrays
Array element types shall reference valid datatypes
Fixed arrays shall define valid cardinality
Validator shall reject invalid array dimensions
Object models shall support fixed record datatypes
Fixed records shall contain ordered fields
Fixed record fields shall reference valid datatypes
Parser shall preserve field ordering
Validator shall reject unresolved field datatype references
Object models shall support variant record datatypes
Variant records shall define a discriminant
Alternative branches shall reference valid datatypes
Parser shall preserve discriminant mappings
Validator shall reject duplicate discriminant alternatives
OMT elements may contain note metadata
Parser shall preserve note content
Serializer shall reproduce note content
Standard MIM content shall be available through the active catalog and MOM request report paths
FOM module merge shall reject conflicting logical time representations
FOM module merge shall combine supported modules into one active federation object model catalog
Schema-level XML conformance shall be validated against the standard schema when full schema validation is implemented
RTI shall support composition of a federation object model from multiple FOM modules
Merge process shall preserve class hierarchy consistency
Merge process shall preserve datatype consistency
Merge process shall preserve dimension consistency
Merge process shall detect conflicting definitions
Merge process shall reject incompatible duplicate definitions
Merge process shall generate a merged FDD representation
Merge process shall support inclusion of the standard MIM
OMT XML documents shall conform to the published XML schema
Parser shall validate XML namespace usage
Parser shall reject schema-invalid XML documents
Serializer shall emit schema-valid XML
Parse to serialize to parse shall preserve semantic equivalence
HLA object models shall be represented using the OMT components defined by the standard
FOM SOM and MIM parsers shall recognize all standard OMT component tables
Validators shall distinguish required optional and conditionally required OMT entries
Object models shall include object model identification information
Parser shall extract object model name and model type identification fields when present
Implementation shall distinguish FOM SOM and standard MIM object model types
Implementation shall preserve identification metadata during parse and serialize round trip
Object models shall define object class hierarchy beneath ObjectRoot
Each object class except ObjectRoot shall have exactly one immediate superclass
Parser shall preserve parent-child object class relationships
Validator shall reject duplicate object class names in the same object class hierarchy
Implementation shall support inherited attributes through superclass traversal
Object models shall define interaction class hierarchy beneath InteractionRoot
Each interaction class except InteractionRoot shall have exactly one immediate superclass
Parser shall preserve parent-child interaction class relationships
Validator shall reject duplicate interaction class names in the same interaction hierarchy
Implementation shall support inherited parameters through superclass traversal
Object models shall identify logical time representation information where required
Parser shall extract time datatype and time factory metadata
RTI integration shall use OMT time representation data to select logical time factories
Object models shall define user-supplied tag datatype information where required
Parser shall extract and preserve user-supplied tag representation
RTI services that carry tags shall validate tag values against the declared tag representation where applicable
Implementation documentation shall use the FOM SOM lexicon consistently when naming object model artifacts
Conformance claims shall distinguish implemented subsets from unimplemented or planned OMT behavior
FOM module merge shall be transactional when rejecting inconsistent logical time representations
Create federation with an explicit MIM shall preserve the requested MIM module as active provenance
Parser shall accept OMT XML interchange documents rooted at objectModel
Resolver shall normalize XML module designators into backend-consumable URLs or file URIs
MIM XML module payloads shall remain available for MOM request and report paths in the active implementation subset
Schema-level XML conformance shall be validated against the standard schema when full schema validation is implemented
